mechanism_narrative: Rising real GDP growth signals expanding economic output and corporate earnings, leading to higher equity valuations and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 40% of quarters with positive GDP growth over a 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate above the natural rate signals weakening labor markets, reducing consumer spending and corporate profits, which depresses equity prices over the near term.
failure_condition: When UNRATE exceeds 4.5%, the S&P 500 20-day return is positive more than half the time over a 2-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is low relative to inflation, real interest rates become negative, encouraging borrowing and risk-taking, which boosts equity prices as investors move out of cash and bonds.
failure_condition: The joint condition yields a median 20-day SPX return of zero or negative over any rolling 2-year period with at least 10 triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained contraction in industrial production indicates declining manufacturing activity and broader economic weakness, which typically precedes negative equity returns over longer horizons as earnings forecasts are revised downward.
failure_condition: In quarters when INDPRO contracts by more than 0.5% month-over-month, the S&P 500 60-day return is positive more than 45% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts above a key threshold reflect robust residential investment and consumer confidence, providing a short-term positive impulse to equities as the market prices in stronger economic momentum.
failure_condition: When HOUST exceeds 1500, the median 5-day S&P 500 return is zero or negative across all instances in the test period.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production trend growth serves as a leading indicator of GDP expansion; rising output supports higher corporate earnings and, over a quarter, translates into positive equity market returns as investors anticipate improved profitability.
failure_condition: The correlation between INDPRO three-month change and subsequent 60-day SPX return is negative or insignificantly positive (r < 0.1) over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong job creation combined with robust retail sales indicates healthy consumer demand and income growth, which supports corporate revenues and drives equity appreciation in the short to medium term.
failure_condition: When both conditions hold, the S&P 500 20-day return is negative in more than 50% of cases over a 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A rising federal funds rate signals monetary tightening, which increases the discount rate on equity cash flows and reduces risk appetite, leading to lower stock prices as investors adjust to higher borrowing costs.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises by 25bp or more over 3 months
failure_condition: During periods when FEDFUNDS has risen by at least 25bp in the past quarter, the S&P 500 20-day return is positive in more than 40% of months.
